title: To nullify certain interagency guidance related to climate-related financial risk management for large financial institutions.
plain_summary: This bill cancels rules from October 2023 that told big banks how to handle money problems caused by climate change. The bill also stops government agencies from making similar rules in the future. This means banks would not have to follow government advice about climate risks to their business. The bill removes requirements for banks to plan for financial problems that might come from things like floods, storms, or other weather events.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Nullification of Interagency Guidance
sections.text: The government guidance document called Principles for Climate-Related Financial Risk Management for Large Financial Institutions will be canceled and have no legal power. This guidance was published on October twenty-fourth, two thousand twenty-three by three government agencies: the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (the group that oversees the nation's central bank), the Comptroller of the Currency (the office that regulates national banks), and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the agency that insures bank deposits). These same agencies cannot create any new guidance that is basically the same as this canceled guidance.
